title: Host RAM census — firmware reservation + standing consumers + transient AI-load snapshot
purpose: Baseline for the RAM-headroom study: quantify the firmware/hardware reservation, standing background consumers, and the memory shape of the host under a concurrent test-gate load, before selecting reclamation levers.

hardware.gpu: Intel Arc 140V iGPU (unified memory — GPU allocations draw from system RAM)

probes: perf counters \Memory\* (composition), \GPU Adapter Memory(*)\Shared Usage and \GPU Process Memory(*)\Shared Usage
Get-Process working-set/private-bytes grouped by process name; Win32_Process command lines
methodology.conditions: Interactive daytime session. A concurrent Claude session was running the standing pytest gate during part of the census — snapshots labeled 'under_load' vs 'near_idle'. All probes read-only; nothing was modified.
methodology.runs: Single-pass census; GPU counters read three times (transience check).

memory_composition_near_idle.note_in_use_includes_other_dev_sessions: pytest gate (~2 GB), 3 Claude sessions (~2.6 GB private), Edge (~3.0 GB private)

under_load_snapshot.note: Captured at 14:47 while another session's build/test load (with a transient full-model GPU load) was live — demonstrates the machine's real ceiling behavior.

gpu_shared_usage_mb.note: The 9.7 GB was a transient model load from the concurrent gate run: on this iGPU, a resident 14B INT4 model occupies ~9.7 GB of system RAM via GPU shared memory, invisible in process working sets.

windows_feature_state.wsearch: Running/Automatic; Classic (user-folders-only) index scope; SearchIndexer 22 MB working set (live probe at census)
windows_feature_state.vbs_note: VBS running; services {3,4}; Credential Guard (1) and HVCI (2) not in the running list; Secure System process ~102 MB WS
windows_feature_state.pagefile: auto-managed, C: allocated 19456 MB, current 5699 MB, peak 11750 MB

not_measured: Decomposition of the 692.8 MB hardware reservation (needs firmware-level tooling; researched separately)
Idle baseline with zero dev sessions running (all snapshots include this session + one concurrent gate-running session)
Secure-kernel/hypervisor RAM cost beyond the visible Secure System process
Memory Compression store size (process not visible from a non-elevated shell; compression is disabled anyway)
Per-lever savings (this census is the before-picture; levers are applied and re-measured separately)
NPU memory residency (embedding model idle-unloaded at census time)
